note: Bounded quantities MINTED by the 2026 JC falsification -- a counterexample does not only kill statements, it births brackets. The falsification is Alpoge's dim-3 counterexample, 'awaiting confirmation' (widely machine-verified, not yet peer-reviewed); every bound below is conditional on that. Same evidence[]/confidence machinery as atlas/gap_map.json (classes computed by tools/validate_jc_crater.py via the gap_map rule, never asserted).

entries.quantity: Minimal dimension n admitting a Keller counterexample (polynomial self-map of C^n, constant nonzero Jacobian determinant, not injective)

evidence.note: UPPER BOUND 3, machine-checked in Lean 4 + mathlib. `jacobian_conjecture_false` refutes the COMMUNITY formalisation (google-deepmind/formal-conjectures, FormalConjectures/Wikipedia/JacobianConjecture.lean, restated VERBATIM so the goalposts are not ours) for k = Q, sigma = Fin 3. The two load-bearing facts -- det J_F = C (-2), and F(0,0,-1/4) = F(1,-3/2,13/2) with the two points distinct -- are proved against mathlib's own MvPolynomial.pderiv and Matrix.det, so this bound no longer rests on any code of ours (it previously rested on verify.py's monomial engine). Axiom audit: depends on [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound] ONLY -- no sorryAx, no gaps. Build: 7984 jobs, exit 0.
upper bound 3: Alpöge's dim-3 map, independently machine-verified here (det == -2 identically; 3 distinct points, one image)

evidence.artifact: van den Essen, Polynomial Automorphisms and the Jacobian Conjecture (2000); Wikipedia 'Jacobian conjecture' (2026-07 revision)
evidence.note: lower bound 2: n=1 is a trivial theorem (constant nonzero derivative => linear); the plane case n=2 remains OPEN -- the dim-3 counterexample yields no information downward in dimension

entries.notes: The entire remaining content of the (former) Jacobian Conjecture is now the question 'is this quantity 2 or 3?'. Equivalent form: is the plane Jacobian Conjecture true? The bracket closes to 2 by exhibiting a planar Keller counterexample, and to 3 by proving JC_2. CONFIDENCE C0 (round 3): earned by the Lean proof, and SCOPED -- the formal proof covers the UPPER bound (a dim-3 Keller counterexample exists) and nothing else. The lower bound 2 is untouched: it is exactly the open plane-Jacobian question, and no formal proof here bears on it. Two external dependencies remain and are NOT settled by formalisation: attribution of the construction (Alpoge's), and whether the community's formal statement faithfully captures the informal conjecture -- the standing caveat of all formalisation.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 from the crater analysis; construction Alpöge 2026-07-19 (external, 'awaiting confirmation' -- not yet peer-reviewed)

entries.quantity: Minimal total degree (max over components) of a Keller counterexample in dimension 3

evidence.note: upper bound 7: this stdlib script certifies, by exact monomial expansion, that Alpöge's map has component total degrees (7, 6, 4) -- map degree 7. (That the map IS a counterexample is certified separately by certificates/jacobian-conjecture/verify.py.)

evidence.artifact: S. S. Wang, A Jacobian criterion for separability, J. Algebra 65 (1980), 453-494
evidence.note: lower bound 3: Wang's theorem -- Keller maps with all components of degree <= 2 are invertible, for every n in characteristic 0; so any counterexample has a component of degree >= 3

entries.notes: The taboo is gone: a counterexample EXISTS at degree 7, so 'find one of degree 3, 4, 5, or 6 in dim 3' is now a legitimate witness hunt with an instant exact verifier (the certificate's engine generalizes to any candidate map + collision pair). Even no-hit searches over structured families yield certified fences. NOTE the classical cubic-homogeneous reductions do NOT bear on this fixed-dimension quantity -- they change dimension.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20; upper bound machine-certified by map_degree.py 2026-07-20; Alpöge construction 'awaiting confirmation'

entries.quantity: Minimal geometric degree (generic fiber size) of a non-injective Keller map, over all dimensions

evidence.note: upper bound 3: exact stdlib certificate that [C(x,y,z):C(f1,f2,f3)] = 3, i.e. the map is generically 3-to-1. Verifies three Groebner relations as identities in Q[x,y,z] after substituting t_i := f_i (a transcription error fails the check, it cannot pass falsely): y satisfies a cubic over C(f), and x, z are C(f)-rational in y, giving degree <= 3; the cubic specializes at (t1,t2)=(1,0) to 2y^3+18y+27t3, linear in t3 and primitive, hence irreducible by Gauss, giving degree >= 3. The one step left to the reader is Gauss's lemma itself.

evidence.artifact: Singular 'vdim' and Macaulay2 'degree' over QQ(t1,t2,t3) (DGX, 2026-07-20; not committed in-repo)
evidence.note: independent cross-check: two separate CAS engines each return dim 0, degree 3 for the ideal (f1-t1,f2-t2,f3-t3). Agreement with the committed certificate, not a substitute for it -- neither run is replayable from this repo.
lower bound 2: a non-injective map has a fiber with >= 2 points, so geometric degree >= 2

entries.notes: Is a generically 2-to-1 Keller map possible? Genuinely new question exposed by the counterexample's 3-to-1 structure, and the bracket is still OPEN: the upper bound 3 is now CERTIFIED (geometric_degree.py; it was a CAS session-log observation when this quantity was minted), but nothing here decides whether some other Keller map achieves 2. An etale self-map of C^n of degree > 1 is necessarily non-proper (the classical simply-connectedness obstruction), which constrains but does not decide degree 2. Note the etale property matters for the count: det(JF) = -2 is a nonzero constant, so fibers carry no multiplicity and 'degree 3' means three honest points.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 from the crater analysis; upper bound machine-certified by geometric_degree.py 2026-07-20 (superseding the session-log fiber count it was minted with); Alpoge construction 'awaiting confirmation'

entries.quantity: Degree of the non-properness (Jelonek asymptotic) set S_F of Alpöge's dim-3 Keller counterexample

evidence.note: exact value 4: S_F = V(E) with E = 27t1^2t3^2 - 18t1t2t3 + 16t1 + t2^3t3 - t2^2, a quartic. The machine part is 18 exact checks -- three annihilator pullback identities (leading coefficients 2, E(t), 8, so only x can escape), the identity disc_Y(G1) = -2916 t1^2 E, two density facts placing no component of V(E) inside the removed loci, three exact one-point fibers ON V(E), the 3-point fiber off it, and 5 planted-failure controls. The degree is read off E, whose defining identity (I2) is transcription-proof: a wrong E fails the identity, it cannot pass falsely.

evidence.artifact: Z. Jelonek, The set of points at which a polynomial map is not proper, Ann. Polon. Math. 58 (1993) 259-266
evidence.note: the classical prose steps (L1)-(L5) the certificate leans on and does NOT machine-check: the root bound, asymptotic set = non-properness set, proper + etale => covering of degree = the certified generic degree 3, closedness of S_F, and disc = 0 => at most 2 distinct roots. This is Jelonek's leading-coefficient method; the value 4 is a property of THIS map, not a literature-transcribed number.

entries.notes: The 'non-properness escape hatch' that BCW 1982 (proper Keller => invertible) leaves open is now an exact equation for the exhibited counterexample: a degree-4 hypersurface, over whose complement F is an unbranched 3-sheeted covering. Bracket is degenerate (lower = upper) because the value is certified exactly, hence status 'closed' rather than 'open'. NOT claimed: irreducibility of E over Q or C (CAS-probed only, no replay path, and deliberately unused -- the density argument is stated per irreducible component so it never needs to know the components); and Phi_x is claimed only to be AN annihilator of x, never the minimal one. Confidence reading: C2 = exactly one replayable machine artifact; the classical lemmas above are prose, not a second implementation.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 (round 3, anatomy); machine-certified by certificates/jc-anatomy/certify_nonproperness.py; CONDITIONAL on the root claim -- Alpöge's construction 'awaiting confirmation', not ours, not peer-reviewed

entries.quantity: Dimension of the complement C^3 \ im(F) of the image of Alpöge's dim-3 Keller counterexample

evidence.note: lower bound 1: F is NOT surjective -- the punctured rational curve gamma(s) = (s^2/12, s, 4/(3s)), s != 0, has EMPTY fiber for every complex s != 0. Certified route: G1 collapses on gamma so y = s/2 is the only candidate, and the slice resultants R12 = -(1/8)s(sx+2)^2, R13 = 3(sx+2)(sx-2)+4 are simultaneously unsatisfiable (R12 = 0 forces sx = -2, where R13 = 4 != 0). An omitted curve has dimension 1, so the complement has dimension >= 1.

independent replication of the SAME lower bound by a different route: check (I6a) shows both non-leading coefficients of Phi_x vanish identically along gamma (exact Laurent identities in s) while -2t3 = -8/(3s) != 0, so the certified annihilator identity (I2) is unsatisfiable there and gamma can have no preimage. Two genuinely different arguments, same omitted curve.

upper bound 2: #F^-1(t) = 3 exactly whenever t1*t2*Q(t) != 0, so every point off the hypersurface {t1*t2*Q = 0} is hit. The complement is therefore contained in that hypersurface and has dimension <= 2.

entries.notes: Is the omitted set exactly the curve gamma (dimension 1), or does it contain a surface piece (dimension 2)? OPEN. What is certified is the pair of inclusions gamma ∩ im(F) = empty and C^3 \ V(E) subset im(F); the exact equality im(F) = C^3 \ gamma is NOT established -- closing it needs a limit argument plus one CAS-level step, and is typed DERIVED-OBSERVATION, do not promote. Observation (CAS-level, used by nothing): gamma is precisely the singular locus of V(E), i.e. the map omits exactly the singular locus of its own Jelonek set. Confidence reading: the C1 here is honest -- the load-bearing lower bound really is replicated by two independent certified arguments (cusp_curve_empty.py and (I6a)); the third receipt carries the upper bound. Keller-ness never implied surjectivity, and an etale map omitting a point must be non-proper, so nothing here is surprising in theory; the explicit certified curve is what is new.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 (round 3, anatomy); both bounds machine-certified by certificates/jc-anatomy/; CONDITIONAL on the root claim -- Alpöge's construction 'awaiting confirmation', not ours, not peer-reviewed

entries.quantity: Number of distinct fiber cardinalities realized by Alpöge's dim-3 Keller counterexample over C^3 (the size of its fiber-count spectrum)

evidence.note: lower bound 3: the spectrum contains {0, 1, 3}. Ten rational anchors, one per stratum of {t1*t2*Q = 0}, each with a complete exact solve accounting for every complex root -- count 3 at (-1/4,0,0) and (0,2,3); count 1 at six anchors on Q = 0; count 0 (EMPTY) at (3,6,2/9) and (1/3,2,2/3) on the cusp curve.

independent replication of the same three values by a different certificate: (W2) exhibits the 3-point fiber over (-1/4,0,0), (W1a/b/c) exhibit exact one-point fibers at three points of V(E) (two on the t2 = 0 slice, one off it), and (I6a) exhibits the empty fibers along gamma.

upper bound 4: [C(x,y,z) : C(f1,f2,f3)] = 3 and det JF = -2 is a nonzero constant, so the map is etale and every fiber has AT MOST 3 honest points (no multiplicity). Cardinalities >= 4 are therefore impossible, and the only value that could still join {0,1,3} is 2 -- so the spectrum has size 3 or 4.

entries.notes: The bracket IS the open question 'does a fiber of size exactly 2 occur anywhere?'. It closes to 3 by proving no fiber has 2 points, and to 4 by exhibiting one. Every probe so far returned 1 on V(E) and 3 off it; the certified bound on V(E) minus the cusp curve is <= 2, but no certificate has ever exhibited a 2 and none excludes one. Recorded as a SIZE (an integer) rather than as the set {0,1,3} because the ledger brackets integers; the underlying object is the spectrum. Boundary: the whole-stratum counts ('count 1 everywhere on Q = 0 off the cusp', the full t1 = 0 and t2 = 0 slices) are certified AT ANCHORS only -- there is no parametrized familywise certificate, and the 'all points' versions are conjectures supported by every probe. Confidence reading: the C1 is honest -- the lower bound's three exhibited cardinalities are independently replicated by two separate certificates built on different arguments.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 (round 3, anatomy); lower bound machine-certified twice independently, upper bound from the round-2 degree certificate; CONDITIONAL on the root claim -- Alpöge's construction 'awaiting confirmation', not ours, not peer-reviewed

entries.quantity: Number of preimages of Alpöge's dim-3 Keller counterexample that escape to infinity over a generic point of the non-properness set S_F = V(E)

evidence.note: ONE COMPOSITE ARGUMENT (deliberately recorded as a single receipt, not two): (i) checks (W1a/b/c) of certify_nonproperness.py certify the fibers over the three EXHIBITED points (-16/27,0,1), (-4/27,0,2), (-1,2,-2) of V(E)\gamma are exactly one point each; (ii) it composes with the covering degree [C(x,y,z):C(f1,f2,f3)] = 3 certified by atlas/jc-crater/geometric_degree.py, giving escape count = 3 - #F^-1(t) = 2 at those anchors. The degree certificate is an INGREDIENT of this one argument, NOT an independent replication at the claimed range, so it is not recorded as a second artifact -- the WS7 rule reserves C1 for independent replications, and inflating it here would overstate. (The degree receipt is recorded in its own right under jc-min-geometric-degree-noninjective.)

evidence.artifact: Z. Jelonek, The set of points at which a polynomial map is not proper, Ann. Polon. Math. 58 (1993) 259-266; H. Bass, E. H. Connell, D. Wright, Bull. AMS 7 (1982) 287-330
evidence.note: the classical prose step turning a fiber-count DROP into an escape TO INFINITY: proper + etale => unbranched covering with constant sheet number, so #fiber < 3 forces a sequence running to infinity. Asserted, not machine-checked.

entries.notes: HONEST BOUNDARY, and the reason the status is 'open' despite a degenerate bracket: the value 2 is certified at THREE EXHIBITED ANCHORS of V(E) minus the cusp curve, not familywise. The word 'generic' in the quantity name is a CAS-level extrapolation (every probe agreed; there is no parametrized certificate), and 'escape' additionally rests on the classical covering lemma above rather than on machine arithmetic. Note also that the count is NOT 2 everywhere on S_F: on the cusp curve gamma subset V(E) the fiber is EMPTY, so all 3 preimages escape -- 2 is the value off gamma. Confidence reading: the computed C1 comes from two distinct machine artifacts that certify DIFFERENT ingredients (the anchor fiber sizes, and the covering degree 3), NOT from independent replication of one claim; read it as 'two machine artifacts', and weigh the prose step separately. CONFIDENCE READING (round 3, orchestrator): recorded C2, not C1. The two certificates behind the value are ingredients of a single composite argument (anchor fiber sizes x covering degree), not two independent replications at the claimed range, which is what the WS7 C1 class means. Both artifacts remain cited inside the single receipt note; nothing was deleted from the record.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 (round 3, anatomy); anchor values machine-certified by certificates/jc-anatomy/certify_nonproperness.py, degree by atlas/jc-crater/geometric_degree.py; genericity NOT certified; CONDITIONAL on the root claim -- Alpöge's construction 'awaiting confirmation', not ours, not peer-reviewed
